name: Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus
archDisplay: Thinker-Talker omnimodal architecture; both Thinker and Talker adopt Hybrid-Attention MoE; RVQ speech tokenizer plus ARIA text-speech interleaving in the Talker; exact parameter/expert configuration undisclosed
note: Flagship tier of the Qwen3.5-Omni family; claims SOTA on 215 audio/audio-visual understanding, reasoning, and interaction subtasks/benchmarks (3 audio-visual, 5 audio, 8 ASR, 156 language-specific S2TT, 43 language-specific ASR benchmarks, per the launch blog). Supports controllable, structured audio/audio-visual captioning down to screenplay-level detail.

results.notes: Table 4 (Text→Text), Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, confirmed against raw HTML table cell S5.T4.3.4.4. Distinct from Qwen3.5-Plus-Instruct (94.3) and Qwen3.5-Omni-Flash (90.0) in the same row.
Table 4 (Text→Text), Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T4.3.9.4 (bold/best score in that row).
Table 4 (Text→Text), STEM section, Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T4.3.14.4. Paper labels it simply 'GPQA' with no subset (diamond/main) specified.

Table 4 (Text→Text), Reasoning section, Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T4.3.16.4. Paper specifically labels this 'LiveCodeBench v6'; catalog has no version-specific slug so mapped to generic livecodebench.
Table 6 (Vision→Text), STEM and Puzzle section, Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T6.3.3.4.
Table 6 (Vision→Text), Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T6.3.6.4. Paper labels row 'Mathvista (mini)' (the standard mini-test split).
Table 6 (Vision→Text), General VQA section, Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T6.3.13.4. Paper labels row 'MMBench_{EN-DEV-v1.1}' (English dev split, v1.1).
Table 6 (Vision→Text), Text Recognition/Document Understanding section, Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T6.3.16.4. Paper labels row 'CharXiv (RQ)' — the reasoning-question subset.
Table 6 (Vision→Text), Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T6.3.18.4. Paper labels row 'AI2D_TEST'.

Table 6 (Vision→Text), Spatial Intelligence section, Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T6.3.24.4. Paper labels row 'RefCOCO(avg)' with no separate RefCOCO+/RefCOCOg row, so this is an average across RefCOCO val/testA/testB splits.
Table 6 (Vision→Text), Video Understanding section, Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column, cell S5.T6.3.28.4. Paper labels row 'VideoMME (w/o sub.)' — without-subtitles setting.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via direct fetch of the official qwen.ai launch blog and the Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report (arXiv:2604.15804, Apr 2026); press corroboration (MarkTechPost, eWeek, AnalyticsVidhya, apidog) consistently places the launch at March 30, 2026, and confirms closed/proprietary status. PRICING CORRECTED: Alibaba Cloud Model Studio's pricing table for qwen3.5-omni-plus (raw HTML confirmed directly, both on alibabacloud.com/help/en/model-studio/model-pricing and mirrored on qwencloud.com) is a 2x2 matrix, not a single input/output pair: Input Text/Image/Video = $1.40/M, Input Audio = $11.00/M, Output Text = $8.30/M, Output Text+Audio (audio-only billed) = $44.00/M. The original record mislabeled the $11.00 Audio-INPUT price as priceOutput; the correct standard text-output price is $8.30/M. priceInput=$1.40 was already correct. Params remain accurately represented as undisclosed (paramsB/activeParamsB: null) -- the technical report states the model 'scales to hundreds of billions of parameters' but gives no exact total/active split. All three benchmark scores were independently verified against Table 4 of the primary arXiv technical report (not just the self-reported blog): MMLU-Pro 85.9, C-Eval 92.0, IFEval 89.7 for the Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus column -- exact match, high confidence. The '215 SOTA subtasks' claim (3 audio-visual + 5 audio + 8 ASR + 156 S2TT + 43 ASR) is corroborated by both the official blog (via MarkTechPost mirror) and the arXiv abstract itself, so it is genuine, self-reported Alibaba framing rather than an unverifiable claim.

SUPPLEMENTARY COVERAGE PASS: Blog post (qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5-omni) yielded nothing usable: it's a client-side-rendered SPA whose raw HTML contains only an SEO keyword list, no article body or benchmark data. All 15 additional results below come from the arXiv technical report (2604.15804), cross-validated by pulling the raw HTML tables directly (not just the AI-summarized fetch) to confirm every score came from the correct "Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus" column — the paper's Tables 4 and 6 report three columns side by side (Qwen3.5-Plus-Instruct, Qwen3.5-Omni-Flash, Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus), and it would be easy to misattribute a Flash or text-only-baseline score to Omni-Plus without this check. The three previously-known values (MMLU-Pro 85.9, C-Eval 92.0, IFEval 89.7) matched exactly during this process, confirming fidelity.

15 new clean-fit results found: 5 from Table 4 (Text→Text: MMLU-Redux, SuperGPQA, IFBench, GPQA, LiveCodeBench) and 10 from Table 6 (Vision→Text: MMMU, MMMU-Pro, MathVision, MathVista, MMBench-EN, CharXiv, AI2D, OCRBench, RefCOCO, VideoMME).

Deliberately NOT mapped despite being real published Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus results, because forcing them into an adjacent slug would misrepresent the benchmark: BFCL-V4 (63.3) — catalog only has bfcl/bfcl-v3, not v4, which uses a different scoring methodology; TAU2Bench (81.0) — the paper reports one undifferentiated score under "General Agent" with no domain breakdown, while the catalog's only tau2 slug is telecom-specific (tau2-bench-telecom), so mapping would falsely imply a telecom-only result; HMMT Nov 25 (84.4) and IMOAnswerBench (65.5) — distinct competition-math benchmarks from AIME/olympiadbench with no catalog slug.

Also genuinely no-fit (checked and excluded, not from laziness): AA-LCR, LongBench v2, DynaMath, ZEROBench/ZEROBench_sub, RealWorldQA, MMStar, SimpleVQA (not the same benchmark as catalog's simpleqa), CC-OCR, MMLongBench-Doc, ERQA, CountBench, ODInW13, EmbSpatialBench, MLVU, MVBench, LVBench, MMVU, MME-VideoOCR, SLAKE, PMC-VQA, MedXpertQA-MM.

Honest bottom line on audio: Tables 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 12 of the report — roughly 100+ additional reported numbers (MMAU, MMAR, MMSU, VoiceBench, URO-Bench, Fleurs, CommonVoice/Librispeech/WenetSpeech/Kespeech WER, DailyOmni, WorldSense, AVUT, OmniGAIA, plus zero-shot/multilingual/cross-lingual TTS WER and speaker-similarity across ~29 languages) — are exhaustively audio, ASR, speech-translation, and speech-generation benchmarks with no analog anywhere in the given slug list. This confirms the task's own hypothesis: Qwen3.5-Omni-Plus's published eval suite is genuinely audio-first, and beyond the 15 text/vision results captured here, there isn't more mappable text-benchmark data for this model — that's a property of what Alibaba chose to report, not a gap in the extraction.
